patterns_checked: reviewed prior migration scripts for v3->v4
key_outputs: schema v4 applies cleanly; live DB still at v3

verification.details: schema v4 applies cleanly and the migration test suite passes

memorialize_suggestions.description: Memory schema v4 splits class/status and introduces memory_links
memorialize_suggestions.body: On 2026-05-22, memory schema advanced to v4. Atoms now carry a `class` field (anchor/thread/log) and a `status` field; structural connections live in the new `memory_links` table rather than being inlined in atom bodies. Migration is idempotent and preserves all v3 rows. The live ~/.gaia/gaia.db is migrated by `gaia install --live` (T9).
memorialize_suggestions.rationale: Closed architectural choice that future memory writers and readers will need to reference; surfaced while validating T8 against the v3->v4 migration test.

memorialize_suggestions.description: Do not encode memory cross-references inside atom bodies
memorialize_suggestions.body: Previously, links between memory atoms were encoded as substrings inside atom bodies (e.g. `see also atom_x`). v4 introduces `memory_links` as the structural channel for these relationships. New writers should populate `memory_links` rows; readers should resolve cross-references through that table rather than parsing body text.
memorialize_suggestions.rationale: Path the workspace should not revisit; closed by the v4 refactor.
